title: Unit 1 – The Weekend Workshop
description: Check-in, Station 1, 2 & 3 – Lifestyle, Community, Stadt & Land

vocab.ex: If we attend the workshop, we'll get extra points for our class project.
He attends an academy for music. They play small roles in real musicals.
Friends usually have a lot in common.
Do you do anything special in your community?
Sorry to bother you, but I need your help.
I'm bored with my room; I want to change something.

People often think of crime when they hear the word gang.

She doesn't give new people a chance because she isn't very open-minded.

Are people jealous of Rishi because his family has lots of money?

vocab.de: nicht interessiert sein an; kein Interesse haben an
vocab.ex: Am I too boring for you? You look so uninterested in our conversation.

Why is it so hard to leave my comfort zone? Maybe because it's so comfortable?

Students aren't allowed to use their phones at some schools.

vocab.en: I can't help the fact that
vocab.ex: I can't help the fact that I'm good at maths.
What is it like outside the big cities in England, in the rural areas?
Rome is the centre of the Catholic Church.

Shall we take a break now?

What hopes do you have for the workshop, Lauren?

I'm often alone, but I don't always feel lonely.

Rishi's parents make a lot of money, so they're successful.
Has Rishi ever experienced what it's like in a council estate?
Before a test she's always a bit nervous.

Not every change is an improvement.
She's unpopular because she writes such negative posts.

Sometimes it's hard to achieve your goals.
